delete_domain_message: Are you sure you want to delete this domain?
saml_settings: Your SAML settings have been saved, please login from incognito window to confirm that it has been set up correctly
members_subtitle: Overview of people added to this workspace.
domain_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the domain <0>{{name}}</0>? This action cannot be undone.
selector_subtitle: DS-APM supports the following single sign-on services (SSO). Get started with setting your project’s SSO below
selector_google_description: Let members sign-in with a Google workspace account
selector_saml_description: Azure, Active Directory, Okta or your custom SAML 2.0 solution
selector_oidc_description: Authenticate using OpenID Connect providers like Azure, Active Directory, Okta, or other OIDC compliant solutions
tooltip_domain: The email domain for users who should use SSO (e.g., `example.com` for users with `@example.com` emails)
tooltip_skip_email_verification: Whether to skip email verification. Defaults to "false"
google_description: Enter OAuth 2.0 credentials obtained from the Google API Console below. Read the <0>docs</0> for more information.
google_tooltip_client_id: ClientID is the application's ID. For example, 292085223830.apps.googleusercontent.com.
google_callout: Google OAuth2 won't be enabled unless you enter all the attributes above
google_workspace_groups_description: Enable group fetching to retrieve user groups from Google Workspace. Requires a Service Account with domain-wide delegation.
google_tooltip_fetch_groups: Enable fetching Google Workspace groups for the user. Requires service account configuration.
google_tooltip_service_account_json: The JSON content of the Google Service Account credentials file. Required for group fetching.
google_tooltip_fetch_transitive_membersh: If enabled, recursively fetch groups that contain other groups (transitive membership).
google_tooltip_allowed_groups: Optional list of allowed groups. If configured, only users belonging to one of these groups will be allowed to login.
google_allowed_groups_placeholder: Type a group email and press Enter
oidc_description: Configure OpenID Connect Single Sign-On with your Identity Provider. Read the <0>docs</0> for more information.
oidc_tooltip_issuer_url: The URL identifier for the OIDC provider. For example: "https://accounts.google.com" or "https://login.salesforce.com".
oidc_tooltip_issuer_alias: Optional: Override the issuer URL from .well-known/openid-configuration for providers like Azure or Oracle IDCS.
oidc_tooltip_client_id: The application's client ID from your OIDC provider.
oidc_tooltip_client_secret: The application's client secret from your OIDC provider.
oidc_tooltip_get_user_info: Use the userinfo endpoint to get additional claims. Useful when providers return thin ID tokens.
oidc_callout: OIDC won't be enabled unless you enter all the attributes above
saml_description: Configure SAML 2.0 Single Sign-On with your Identity Provider. Read the <0>docs</0> for more information.
saml_tooltip_acs_url: The SSO endpoint of the SAML identity provider. It can typically be found in the SingleSignOnService element in the SAML metadata of the identity provider.
saml_tooltip_entity_id: The entityID of the SAML identity provider. It can typically be found in the EntityID attribute of the EntityDescriptor element in the SAML metadata.
saml_tooltip_certificate: The certificate of the SAML identity provider. It can typically be found in the X509Certificate element in the SAML metadata.
saml_tooltip_skip_signing: Whether to skip signing the SAML requests. For providers like JumpCloud, this should be enabled.
saml_callout: SAML won't be enabled unless you enter all the attributes above
attr_mapping_description: Configure how SAML assertion attributes from your Identity Provider map to DS-APM user attributes. Leave empty to use default values.
attr_mapping_tooltip_email: The SAML attribute key that contains the user's email. Default: 'email'
attr_mapping_tooltip_name: The SAML attribute key that contains the user's display name. Default: 'name'
attr_mapping_tooltip_groups: The SAML attribute key that contains the user's group memberships. Used for role mapping. Default: 'groups'
attr_mapping_tooltip_role: The SAML attribute key that contains the user's role directly from the IDP. Default: 'role'
claim_mapping_description: Configure how claims from your Identity Provider map to DS-APM user attributes. Leave empty to use default values.
claim_mapping_tooltip_email: The claim key that contains the user's email address. Default: 'email'
claim_mapping_tooltip_name: The claim key that contains the user's display name. Default: 'name'
claim_mapping_tooltip_groups: The claim key that contains the user's group memberships. Used for role mapping. Default: 'groups'
claim_mapping_tooltip_role: The claim key that contains the user's role directly from the IDP. Default: 'role'
domain_mapping_description: Map workspace domains to admin emails for service account impersonation. Use "*" as a wildcard for any domain.
role_mapping_description: Configure how user roles are determined from your Identity Provider. You can either use a direct role attribute or map IDP groups to DS-APM roles.
role_mapping_tooltip_default_role: The default role assigned to new SSO users if no other role mapping applies. Default: "VIEWER"
role_mapping_tooltip_use_role_attribute: If enabled, the role claim/attribute from the IDP will be used directly instead of group mappings. The role value must match a DS-APM role (VIEWER, EDITOR, or ADMIN).
role_mapping_group_description: Map IDP group names to DS-APM roles. If a user belongs to multiple groups, the highest privilege role will be assigned.
member_tooltip_root_user: This operation is not supported for the root user
member_tooltip_self_delete: You cannot perform this action on your own account
member_tooltip_self_role: You cannot modify your own role
member_reset_link_failed: Failed to generate password reset link
member_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete <0>{{name}}</0>? This will remove their access to the workspace.
member_revoke_invite_confirm: Are you sure you want to revoke the invite for <0>{{email}}</0>? They will no longer be able to join the workspace using this invite.
reset_dialog_invite_desc: Share this one-time link with the team member to complete their account setup.
reset_dialog_reset_desc: This creates a one-time link the team member can use to set a new password for their DS-APM account.
